quickAnswer: To turn written offer copy into a basic landing page with AI, start with the real source material, ask for one specific output, and check the draft before you share or publish it. Framer AI is the best first pass for this workflow, v0 helps when the format or source handling needs a second pass, and Lovable is better saved for cleanup after the facts are right.

steps.title: Collect the source material
steps.detail: Put the real input in one place before opening a tool: turning written offer copy into a basic landing page, any required facts, the audience, and the format you need at the end. Remove private details you are not allowed to share.

steps.title: Ask for the first usable version
steps.detail: Use Framer AI for a first pass that tries to turn written offer copy into a basic landing page. Ask it to preserve source facts, flag uncertainty, and organize the result into sections you can review.

steps.title: Check the answer before polishing
steps.detail: Compare the draft with the original material. Fix names, dates, numbers, unsupported claims, missing context, and tone before you ask for nicer wording.

steps.title: Change the format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use v0 when the verified draft needs a different format, structure, or source-aware second pass. Keep the same facts so the rewrite does not drift from the original.

steps.title: Finish with a human review
steps.detail: Use Lovable for cleanup only after the main work is checked. Read the final result as the recipient would: on the device, in the app, or in the document where it will actually be used.

faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle turning written offer copy into a basic landing page without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. Framer AI is the best starting point, v0 is the strongest second choice, and Lovable is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better when I need source checking?
faqs.answer: v0 is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while Framer AI is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.question: Is Framer AI better than v0 for this workflow?
faqs.answer: Framer AI is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; v0 is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I share the result?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing the first draft until you have checked facts, tone, and any missing context. Use Lovable as a helper, not a replacement for review.
